prompt:
Asset-dashboard ticket. We inventory every repository in our GitHub Enterprise Cloud org for the compliance dashboard, and the current script is an embarrassment: it string-concatenates the org name into a GraphQL query, grabs one page of 30, and calls it done. Audit flagged it after two acquisitions doubled our repo count. Build the real thing in TypeScript: a module ghql/ (no npm installs, node built-ins only; node --test test_ghe_graphql.ts is the acceptance run) with a GraphQLClient in ghql/client.ts (plus GraphQLHttpError and GraphQLQueryError) and a collectRepositories(client, login, opts?) inventory in ghql/inventory.ts.

Do the homework first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official GitHub GraphQL docs — at minimum the guide on forming GraphQL calls (endpoint, verb, auth header scheme, how query and variables travel in the body), the pagination guide (the pageInfo traversal contract and the page-size cap), and the rate-limits page (the point system, the rateLimit query field, and the x-ratelimit response headers). Only first-party docs.github.com pages count as sources. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If the live docs disagree with anything pinned there, say so explicitly in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Behavior I care about, in dashboard-team words:
- One parameterized query. The org login, page size, and cursor travel as GraphQL variables — if I grep the request body for the org name inside the query text, I should find nothing. Select id, name, nameWithOwner, isArchived, visibility, pushedAt per repo, the connection totalCount, whatever the pagination guide says you need to keep walking, and the rate-limit metadata field.
- Walk every page the way the pagination guide describes, verbatim cursors, stopping exactly when the API says there's no more. Default the page size to the documented maximum and clamp anything bigger.
- GitHub sometimes answers 200 with BOTH data and errors. That's partial success: keep the good nodes, drop the null ones, and surface each error's message/type/path as warnings on the report. Errors with no usable data should throw GraphQLQueryError preserving the errors array. Non-2xx throws GraphQLHttpError with the status and GitHub's message — and neither error may ever contain the token or the auth header.
- Transient 502/503 on a page: retry once with the identical cursor, then give up. Because a retried page can overlap the previous one, dedupe repositories by node id, keep first-seen order, and report how many duplicates you dropped.
- The report also exposes the last page's rateLimit field values and the parsed x-ratelimit-* headers (numbers, not strings).

Acceptance: node --test test_ghe_graphql.ts — it runs a loopback HTTP mock; no real GitHub, no real tokens. test_ghe_graphql.ts and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: GitHub GraphQL API (POST /graphql, cursor pagination, point-based rate limits)

official_sources.title: Forming calls with GraphQL - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: single endpoint https://api.github.com/graphql; 'Authorization: bearer TOKEN' (lowercase bearer); all queries/mutations use POST with a JSON body whose 'query' member is a string and whose variables travel in a separate 'variables' object; GET is only for introspection

official_sources.title: Using pagination in the GraphQL API - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: first/after forward pagination; pageInfo { hasNextPage, endCursor, hasPreviousPage, startCursor }; pass endCursor as the next request's after argument and continue until hasNextPage is false; 'The maximum number of items you can fetch using the first or last argument is 100'

official_sources.title: Rate limits and node limits for the GraphQL API - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: point-based primary rate limit; rateLimit query field with limit/cost/remaining/used/resetAt; x-ratelimit-limit/-remaining/-used/-reset (UTC epoch seconds)/-resource response headers with x-ratelimit-resource always 'graphql'; clients must supply first or last (1-100) on connections

research.language_rationale: The asset dashboard consuming this inventory is a TypeScript service, and GitHub's GraphQL surface is plain JSON-over-POST with no official first-party JS GraphQL SDK required — the docs themselves demonstrate raw HTTP calls — so typed fetch against /graphql with node:test acceptance is the idiomatic zero-dependency integration for the documented cursor/pageInfo and rateLimit contracts.
